section_title: An agent who is guilty of misconduct in the business of the agency is
content:
220. An agent who is guilty of misconduct in the business of the agency is
entitled to
not entitled to any remuneration in respect of that part of the business which
remuneration
for business he has misconducted.
mis-
Illustrations
conducted
(a) A employs B to recover 1,00,000 Taka from C, and to lay it out on good
security. B recovers the 1,00,000 Taka and lays out 90,000 Taka on good
security, but lays out 10,000 Taka on security which he ought to have known
to be bad, whereby A loses 2,000 Taka. B is entitled to remuneration for
recovering the 1,00,000 Taka and for investing the 90,000 Taka. He is not
entitled to any remuneration for investing the 10,000 Taka, and he must
make good the 2,000 Taka to B.
(b) A employs B to recover 1,000 Taka from C. Through B's misconduct the
money is not recovered. B is entitled to no remuneration for his services, and
must make good the loss.
Agent's lien

A employs B to recover 1,00,000 Taka from C, and to lay it out on good
security. B recovers the 1,00,000 Taka and lays out 90,000 Taka on good
security, but lays out 10,000 Taka on security which he ought to have known
to be bad, whereby A loses 2,000 Taka. B is entitled to remuneration for
recovering the 1,00,000 Taka and for investing the 90,000 Taka. He is not
entitled to any remuneration for investing the 10,000 Taka, and he must
make good the 2,000 Taka to B.

A employs B to recover 1,000 Taka from C. Through B's misconduct the
money is not recovered. B is entitled to no remuneration for his services, and
must make good the loss.
Agent's lien
